title: Akanseh
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Character |type= |image=[[File:Akanseh.jpg]] |name=Akanseh |homeworld=[[Dac/Legends|Dac]]''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' |birth= |death= |species=[[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him{{DB|character|akanseh|Akanseh, Doctor}} |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes=[[Color/Legends|Black]] |skin=Orange-brown |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]]'s [[Moff Sarne's forces|forces]] {{C|Unwillingly}} *''[[FarStar]]'' |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|What were you charged with?''"
"''Moff Sarne seldom needed anything so mundane as a charge.|Lieutenant Judder Page and Doctor Akanseh|{{DarkStryder|adventure=The Saga Begins|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}}}} '''Akanseh''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Doctor/Legends|doctor]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dac/Legends|Dac]]. A philanthropist and accomplished surgeon, he wanted to use his skills to take medical care to the backwater worlds of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. When the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] [[Subjugation of Mon Calamari|invaded]] his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], Akanseh fled and avoided becoming a [[Slavery/Legends|slave]]. Hiding for several [[Standard year/Legends|years]], he built a fully automated surgical suite staffed by [[Droid/Legends|droids]] and set out to achieve his goals. Since the surgical suite could be broken down and stowed in the hold of a [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]], Akanseh used [[free-trader]]s to transport the apparatus from world to world. While being ferried between planets, the freighter Akanseh had booked passage on was intercepted by an [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Interdictor cruiser]]. The doctor was taken to [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]], an Imperial [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] who ruled the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]]. Sarne saw that Akanseh's talents and surgical suite would serve him well, and forced the Mon Calamari to work for him as an [[Interrogator (occupation)|interrogator]] and [[Torture/Legends|torturer]]. Akanseh did his best to act humanely in this role, using persuasion and coercion to gain his subject's secrets without resorting to violence. Despite this, he still used more forceful methods on occasion. In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], he was freed from his servitude to the Moff by [[Katarn Commandos|Page's Commandos]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] during the [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol]]. Moff Sarne had fled [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]] with the bulk of his [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]], and the New Republic [[CR90 corvette/Legends|corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'' had been assigned to [[DarkStryder Campaign|track him down]] and neutralize any threat he posed. Akanseh signed onto the crew of the ''FarStar'' as [[chief medical officer]], hoping to atone for his actions while in Sarne's service. Several [[Standard month/Legends|months]] into the voyage, the [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] stopped at the planet [[Q'Maere]] in the [[Kathol Rift/Legends|Kathol Rift]]. The planet was the site of the [[Q'Maere Research Facility]], a former [[Scientist/Legends|scientific]] outpost that had been converted into a [[Prison/Legends|penal colony]] by Moff Sarne. Many of the [[Prisoner/Legends|inmates]] had been poorly and brutally treated, and Akanseh elected to stay behind at the outpost to care for them. ==Biography== ===Early life=== An accomplished [[Doctor/Legends|surgeon]] and philanthropist on [[Dac/Legends|Dac]], the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] Akanseh dreamed of providing advanced medical services to the unprivileged backwater [[Planet/Legends|planets]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], and aiding those who needed it the most. Before he could realize his goal, the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] [[Subjugation of Mon Calamari|subjugated]] the planet. Akanseh fled his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], and avoided becoming one of the many Mon Calamari [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Akanseh spent several [[Standard year/Legends|years]] in hiding, developing a mobile surgical suite to fulfill his dream of providing medical services to the needy. The suite was largely staffed by [[Medical droid/Legends|medical droids]] programmed by Akanseh himself, and the doctor planned for them to conduct routine operations on their own while he supervised. The unit was designed to be able to fit into the hold of a [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]], and could also be assembled to create a field surgery unit. Around the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], Akanseh completed his surgical suite, and began traveling to backwater planets, where he hoped to aid underprivileged individuals in need. He traveled in [[free-trader]] [[Starship/Legends|ships]], trading his medical services for full passage, or at least a reduced price. While en route to a new planet, the freighter Akanseh was traveling in was captured by an Imperial [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Interdictor cruiser]]. The Mon Calamari was taken to [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]], and brought before [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]], an Imperial [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] and ruler of the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]]. ===Working for Moff Sarne=== {{Quote|If you do not cooperate with me, Moff Sarne will most definitely draw the information he wants from you using drugs…or worse.|Akanseh, to a captive resistance fighter|{{DarkStryder|adventure=Spot Inspection|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}}}} [[File:KentorSarne.png|left|thumb|Moff Kentor Sarne]] Sarne confiscated Akanseh's medical suite, and saw that the doctor's medical skills and knowledge of [[Droid/Legends|droids]] and droid programming would serve him well in his dungeons as an [[Interrogator (occupation)|interrogator]]. A peaceful being, Akanseh chose not to resist and became a submissive [[Prison/Legends|prisoner]], carrying out Sarne's orders. However, Akanseh had his own methods. Rather than [[Torture/Legends|torturing]] the prisoners for information, he persuaded his subjects to trust him enough to disclose the information willingly. He did not want to cause other beings harm, and he knew that if he couldn't get the information in this manner, then the prisoner would be turned over to Imperial interrogators and droids. His techniques were effective, and his rate of success was noted by the Moff. Akanseh once interrogated a stubborn [[Unidentified resistance fighter (Kal'Shebbol)|resistance fighter]], attempting to get the young [[Human/Legends|Human]] to reveal the location of his compatriots who were causing havoc at Kal'Shebbol's [[Spaceport/Legends|starport]]. Treating the rebel's injuries, he tried to persuade the prisoner to see sense and tell him what he knew. Unfortunately for Akanseh, the prisoner was defiant and refused his requests. Desperate not to inflict pain on him, the doctor pleaded with the rebel, telling him that Sarne would use much worse techniques on him if he didn't. At that point, Sarne entered the interrogation room. Having heard about the success that Akanseh was having with his subjects, the Moff had decided to supervise one of his sessions. After observing Akanseh's failure at persuading the subject to talk, Sarne presented the Mon Calamari with a [[hypo-syringe]] and a [[Unidentified clear liquid|clear liquid]] that, when injected into a subject, would make their [[Blood/Legends|blood]] boil. Akanseh hesitated to use the chemical substance, and Sarne noticed. The Moff revealed he had a second hypo-syringe, and commented that if the doctor was unsuccessful, then he would suffer the same fate as the prisoner. Despite working for Sarne as an interrogator, Akanseh was still considered to be a prisoner, and was kept in the [[Detention area|detention block]] in the [[Imperial Government Complex|Moff's palace]]. In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]],The events of ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' are set in [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]] according to ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''. the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|liberated]] the planet. [[Soldier/Legends|Soldiers]] from [[Katarn Commandos|Page's Commandos]], led by [[Kaiya Adrimetrum]], freed all the prisoners in the detention area as part of their invasion of Sarne's headquarters. ===Service aboard the ''FarStar''=== {{Quote|The operating facilities are a little skimpy, but Doctor Akanseh says we can make up a lot of that with his medical droids.''"
"''If they still exist. Moff Sarne confiscated my entire mobile surgical suite when he put me in detention.|Keleman Ciro and Akanseh discuss the ''[[FarStar]]''{{'s}} medical facilities|}} [[File:FarStar.jpg|right|thumb|The ''FarStar'']] Freed from imprisonment, Akanseh learned of a New Republic [[DarkStryder Campaign|mission]] assigned to track Moff Sarne down. The Moff had escaped from Kal'Shebbol with the majority of his [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]], and the modified New Republic [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'' had been charged to pursue and neutralize him. The doctor signed onto the ''FarStar'' mission, seeing it as penance for the acts he performed in Sarne's service. However, while Akanseh admitted he did some medical work for the Moff, he did not reveal the nature of the work to [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Judder Page/Legends|Judder Page]], the New Republic [[Officer/Legends|officer]] in charge of putting the mission together. One of the first to join the mission, Akanseh was part of the inspection team that checked out the ''FarStar'' at its secret berth in the [[Sorbiss Valley]]. He inspected the vessel's [[Medical bay/Legends|medical facilities]] along with [[Keleman Ciro]] and Kaiya Adrimetrum, pronouncing them "reasonably complete." As the [[chief medical officer]] on the ''FarStar'', Akanseh was responsible for overseeing the medical bay, keeping track of medical supplies, and maintaining the medical droid pool. He ran the medical facility well, and it was always brightly lit, warm and stocked with hot [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]]. The crew often joked about getting injured just so they could spend a few comfortable [[Standard day/Legends|days]] in sickbay. Akanseh's droid programming skills also came in useful since the medical droids had to be well suited to deal with a crew composed of a diverse number of [[Species/Legends|species]]. His ultimate goal was to recreate his surgical suite, but he realized that, since that was not part of the ''FarStar''{{'s}} mission, it could not take priority. However, he worked in secret during his time on the ship, assembling equipment and medical droids. [[File:FarStar_delegation.jpg|left|thumb|The command crew of the ''FarStar'' are welcomed by Imperial officials on the planet Gandle Ott.]] Shortly after launching from Kal'Shebbol, the ''FarStar'' intercepted an [[Distress signal/Legends|emergency call]] from the planet [[Kolatill]]. Its [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]], [[Domaz]], had been [[Bombing of Domaz|attacked]] by [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE bombers]] in retaliation for the overthrow of the planet's Imperial [[Governor/Legends|governor]]. The ''FarStar'' arrived in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] several days later, and Akanseh led the medical relief teams to the surface to help the injured.{{DarkStryder|adventure=Omens|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}} Shortly after, he was part of the ''FarStar''{{'s}} delegation that met with the [[Politics/Legends|political]] leaders of the planet [[Gandle Ott]],{{DarkStryder|adventure=Death Is Remotely Possible|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}} and also a member of the party that was greeted by Imperial [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Heget]] at [[Imperial Depot 4290-1]] on [[Shintel]].{{DarkStryder|adventure=Shintel Downtime|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}} ===Treating Captain Adrimetrum=== {{Quote|We've discovered no known cause for her condition, so finding out why she's in this coma will take a lot longer. And she's not even responding to bacta treatment. Her wounds are healing rapidly, but she just won't wake up. Quite frankly, we are running out of ideas.|Akanseh updates the ''FarStar'' crew on Captain Adrimetrum's condition|{{DarkStryder|adventure=Traitor in our Midst|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}}}} Several [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] into their voyage, the ''FarStar'' made a brief layover at [[Galtea/Legends|Galtea]], gathering supplies and performing maintenance before crossing the empty expanse of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] known as the [[Marcol Void/Legends|Marcol Void]].''[[The Kathol Outback]]'' An Imperial [[Agent/Legends|agent]], known as [[the Wraith]], had joined the crew of the ship at [[Pembric II]], posing as a man named Drenn. He had come aboard with [[Breslin Drake]], a former [[New Republic Intelligence Service]] agent who was managing the [[ThrusterBurn Tapcafe]], after running afoul of the local [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]], [[Crev Bombaasa/Legends|Crev Bombaasa]]. However, Drake had no idea that Drenn was an Imperial [[Spy/Legends|spy]], and was, in fact, searching for him.{{DarkStryder|adventure=Crisis (adventure)|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}} [[File:FarStar_bridge_boom.jpg|thumb|right|The ''FarStar'' is attacked by Imperial forces.]] The Wraith used the ''FarStar''{{'s}} communications system to contact an Imperial patrol and arrange for it to attack the [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]]. He then rigged one of the [[K4 security droid]]s on the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] with a miniature [[Projectile launcher/Legends|projectile launcher]] loaded with a [[Poison dart/Legends|poison dart]], as well as a [[Shaped charge/Legends|shaped charge]] on a [[Power conduit/Legends|power conduit]] near the [[Captain/Legends|captain's]] station. While landed on the surface, the corvette's [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]] detected the approach of several Imperial ships. Adrimetrum gave the order to launch into orbit and [[Space warfare/Legends|engage]] the enemy. During the ensuing [[Battle of Galtea|battle]], Adrimetrum was apparently injured following an explosion on the bridge and fell into a [[Coma/Legends|coma]]. In reality, the Wraith's shaped charge gave the appearance that Adrimetrum had been injured during the battle, covering the fact that she had been shot with a [[Poison/Legends|toxic]] dart. Examining her in the medical bay, Akanseh could find no cause for her condition—the captain's injuries consisted of several cuts, a broken [[Limb/Legends|arm]], broken ribs and bruising, and none of them was responsible for the coma. An investigation was launched into the matter, led by the [[Defel/Legends|Defel]] [[Kl'aal]]. After reviewing security footage from the command center taken during the battle, it was realized that the explosion that caused the captain's injuries did not correspond to any weapon strikes against the ship. Further analysis showed that the [[memory buffer]] on one of the K4 [[Security droid/Legends|security droids]] on the bridge had been altered, and that Adrimetrum had apparently slapped at her neck just prior to the explosion. Aware of these new findings, Akanseh managed to locate a small puncture wound at the base of the captain's neck, and a decrease in neurotransmitter activity in her [[Brain/Legends|brain]]. The doctor discovered that a contact poison was to blame for the captain's condition, but the substance did not appear in any of the medical databases. [[File:Wraithspy.jpg|thumb|left|Wraith, the Imperial spy responsible for Captain Adrimetrum's injuries.]] Drake recognized the effects as being similar to those of a [[Sebiris poison|toxin]] used by the Imperial agent known only as the Wraith. He believed that the toxin originated from the planet [[Sebiris]], but had never found the source of it on his trips to the planet. The ''FarStar'' headed to Sebiris, and a landing party met with the native inhabitants of the planet, the [[Sebiri]]. The [[Unidentified Sebiri headman|headman]] of a local tribe informed the landing party that the only cure to the toxin was a [[Unidentified herb|herb]] that grew near one of their [[Stone temple|sacred grounds]]. The landing party gave the [[headman]] gifts, including luxury food items, and he personally escorted them to the site with twelve of his warriors. During their trek into the jungle to the sacred site, one of the Sebiri referred a member of the landing party called Drenn by the name "Petrivoor," an alias that Drake knew the Wraith was known to use. Drake could not believe that the man he had been searching for had been right under his nose, and a brief fight ensued. The Wraith escaped, but it was clear to the crew that he had engineered Adrimetrum's injury. The landing party managed to recover several of the herbs, and returned to the ''FarStar''. Initially, Akanseh was skeptical about the healing properties of the primitive cure, but was amazed when the captain came out her coma after he administered it. After crossing the Marcol Void, the ''FarStar'' made a stopover at the planet [[Binaros]] to take on food supplies. The landing party inadvertently brought back the spores of a [[feeder plant]], which took root and began to grow within the ship's lower forward hold. The plant utilized mobile feeders to gather food and nutrients for itself, and also required [[Nitrogen/Legends|nitrogen]] taken from the blood of living animals. One of the feeders attacked and killed a member of the crew for the nitrogen within their blood stream. Akanseh examined the dead crewmember, and pronounced that he had been completely drained of blood. The feeder plant was stopped by a gaseous poison developed by [[Varel DeVay]], the corvette's [[Xenobiologist/Legends|xenobiologist]].{{DarkStryder|adventure=Plant Food|book=The Kathol Outback}} ===Redemption=== [[File:Qmaereresearchfacility.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Q'Maere Research Facility]] Several [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after leaving Kal'Shebbol, the ''FarStar'' entered the [[Kathol Rift/Legends|Kathol Rift]], an area of [[Nebula/Legends|volatile gases]] plagued by dense [[lightstorm]]s.''[[The Kathol Rift]]'' A [[Variant|bacteria mutation]] within the Rift rotted the corvette's food stores, forcing them to stop at the planet [[Q'Maere]] within the Rift itself. According to records that the ''FarStar'' had obtained, Q'Maere supported a [[Scientist/Legends|scientific]] [[Q'Maere Research Facility|outpost]]. However, unknown to the crew, Moff Sarne had secretly taken the facility over and converted it into a penal colony and re-education center.{{DarkStryder|adventure=Harm's Way|book=The Kathol Rift}} [[Standard week/Legends|Weeks]] before the ''FarStar''{{'s}} arrival, Moff Sarne had stripped the facility of its useful personnel and security [[Troop/Legends|troops]], and stranded the remaining staff and all of the prisoners. The facility's director, [[Langstyn Kraay]], was desperate for a way off the planet, and took a [[Mission group|landing party]] from the ''FarStar'' hostage. He hoped that by holding the delegation to ransom, the crew of the ''FarStar'' would surrender the ship to him. The landing party was put in with the main prison population, and, together with a New Republic prisoner named [[Lowen Chase]], they staged a jailbreak and took control of the facility. Despite being freed, the ''FarStar'' could only take a small number of the prisoners aboard, and the rest would have to remain at the facility. Akanseh, seeing a chance to atone for his past transgressions, volunteered to stay behind at the outpost and care for those patients that had suffered at the hands of Sarne's torturers. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|It seems your persuasive manner will not be enough to sway this young fool, Akanseh.|Moff Kentor Sarne, to Akanseh|}} [[File:FarStar-Shintel.jpg|thumb|left|The command crew of the ''FarStar'' meeting the Imperial forces on the planet Shintel.]] Akanseh was a kind and generous Mon Calamari with [[Color/Legends|orange]]-brown skin and black [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and had a knack for getting other beings to trust him, even in dire situations. He believed in helping the helpless, and was insistent in bringing medical services to backwater planets, even while serving aboard the ''FarStar''. To him, withholding medical services was just as bad as causing injury. Although a talented shipboard doctor, he was easily confused in combat situations and was not an effective combat medic. Akanseh was generally peaceful and non-violent, although he was forced to betray his beliefs when he was captured by Moff Sarne. He was deeply ashamed of his time as an interrogator, and simply wanted to forget about it. His greatest fear was that someone would find out about his past and expose him, and he had no compunctions about lying to cover up his past deeds. ==Skills and abilities== Akanseh had knowledge of [[Alien/Legends|alien]] species and was learned in medicine, able to perform complex surgery and operate [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]] tanks. His experience with using a droid automated surgical suite gave him expertise in [[Computer/Legends|computer]] programming and repair, as well in droid programming, especially in regards to [[MD-series medical droid/Legends|Emdee-series droids]]. He was also able to repair droids that malfunctioned. As a Mon Calamari, Akanseh was amphibious, able to breath in and out of [[Water/Legends|water]]. He was also most happy in moist environments, and dry environments tended to depress him, causing him to seem withdrawn. ==Equipment== Although trained to use a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]], Akanseh did not typically carry one, preferring to only carry [[Medpac/Legends|medpacs]] and a [[Surgical kit|surgical field kit]]. Prior to his capture by Moff Sarne, Akanseh owned and operated a mobile medical suite staffed by droids which could be broken down and carried in the hold of a freighter. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:TheDarkStryderCampaign.jpg|thumb|right|The cover of ''The DarkStryder Campaign''.]] Akanseh was created for ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' by [[West End Games]] for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'', which was published on [[July 17]], [[1995]].{{BN|url=w/star-wars-the-roleplaying-game-box-set/1018175954|text=Star Wars the Roleplaying Game: Darkstryder Campaign Boxed Set}} In the "Crew Notes" section for ''The DarkStryder Campaign'', it was noted that Akanseh should find the redemption he sought during the adventure by committing a truly heroic act, such as sacrificing himself in an effort to save others. However, the developers also stated that the gamemaster and players were under no obligation to eliminate Akanseh from their campaigns. In ''[[The Kathol Rift]]'', Akanseh was written out of the series by stating that, if the players had allowed him to survive to this point in their campaign, then he would remain at the Q'Maere Research Facility to care for those who suffered under Sarne's interrogations. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Akanseh}} *{{DarkStryder|adventure=The Saga Begins|book=The DarkStryder Campaign|reprint=yes}} {{1st}} *{{DarkStryder|adventure=Omens|book=The DarkStryder Campaign|reprint=yes}} *{{DarkStryder|adventure=Death Is Remotely Possible|book=The DarkStryder Campaign|reprint=yes}} {{Po}} *{{DarkStryder|adventure=Shintel Downtime|book=The DarkStryder Campaign|reprint=yes}} *{{DarkStryder|adventure=Traitor in our Midst|book=The DarkStryder Campaign|reprint=yes}} *{{DarkStryder|adventure=Spot Inspection|book=The DarkStryder Campaign|reprint=yes}} *{{DarkStryder|adventure=Plant Food|book=The Kathol Outback|reprint=yes}} *{{DarkStryder|adventure=Harm's Way|book=The Kathol Rift|reprint=yes}} ==Sources== *''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' *''[[The Kathol Outback]]'' *{{GalaxyCite|6|Iron Man}} {{Po}} *''[[The Kathol Rift]]'' *''[[The DarkStryder Campaign, Deluxe]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{DB|character|akanseh|Akanseh, Doctor}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Akanseh#legends |deFA=1 }} [[Category:FarStar crewmembers and personnel]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Medical professionals]] [[Category:Mon Calamari]] [[Category:Personnel of Moff Sarne's forces]]
